message:
fix(fcp-oauth): PKCE verifier wins over extra_token_params (br-fwcci)

crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs — in exchange_code_internal the Pkce
argument inserted `code_verifier` first and then the extra_token_params
loop unconditionally overwrote it via params.insert(key, value.clone()).
A stale / attacker-influenced `code_verifier` entry in
extra_token_params would therefore replace the honest verifier produced
by the authorization flow. Combined with the exchange_code PKCE gate
that treats presence of the key in extra_token_params as satisfying
PKCE, this is a latent bypass of RFC 7636 proof-of-possession.

Fix: merge extra_token_params BEFORE the Pkce insert so the honest
verifier always wins on conflict. One-line reordering plus an
explanatory comment and a regression test.

Regression test: test_exchange_code_pkce_wins_over_extra_token_params_
code_verifier plants a bogus `code_verifier` in extra_token_params,
calls exchange_code_with_pkce with a freshly-generated Pkce, and
asserts the mock token server receives the real Pkce.verifier() value
(wiremock body_string_contains + .expect(1)).

Verified:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline -p fcp-oauth
  -> 611 lib + 15 integration + 59 no-mock tests passed; 0 failed.

Closes flywheel_connectors-fwcci.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-2u02v","title":"[SEC-AUDIT] fcp-oauth OAuth2Client accepts unvalidated redirect_uri","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:326-353 only Url::parse() validates redirect_uri, so OAuth2Client accepts insecure or structurally invalid callback targets (e.g. http://attacker.example/cb, embedded credentials, fragments, query-bearing registered redirects) even though crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs already defines the secure/loopback-only validator. Attack scenario: a connector that forwards operator- or request-supplied redirect URIs through OAuth2Config can mint an authorization URL that sends the provider callback to an attacker-controlled endpoint and leaks the auth code or refreshable session. Proposed mitigation: call normalize_registered_redirect_uri() from OAuth2Client::new/with_http_client (or in with_redirect_uri) so the generic client rejects non-https/non-loopback redirect URIs and malformed registered redirect shapes before any authorization URL is built.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:58:33.976890Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:02:12.218101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-2u02v","title":"[SEC-AUDIT] fcp-oauth OAuth2Client accepts unvalidated redirect_uri","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:326-353 only Url::parse() validates redirect_uri, so OAuth2Client accepts insecure or structurally invalid callback targets (e.g. http://attacker.example/cb, embedded credentials, fragments, query-bearing registered redirects) even though crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs already defines the secure/loopback-only validator. Attack scenario: a connector that forwards operator- or request-supplied redirect URIs through OAuth2Config can mint an authorization URL that sends the provider callback to an attacker-controlled endpoint and leaks the auth code or refreshable session. Proposed mitigation: call normalize_registered_redirect_uri() from OAuth2Client::new/with_http_client (or in with_redirect_uri) so the generic client rejects non-https/non-loopback redirect URIs and malformed registered redirect shapes before any authorization URL is built.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:58:33.976890Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:15:24.631995Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:15:24.631720Z","close_reason":"Validate redirect_uri in OAuth2Client constructors","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2ukh","title":"TEST: Crypto Primitives Unit Tests (Ed25519, X25519, HPKE, AEAD, COSE)","description":"# TEST: Crypto Primitives Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove crypto primitives and encodings are correct via golden vectors + negative tests (Ed25519/X25519/HPKE/AEAD/COSE) without panics or side-channels in logs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for all cryptographic primitives used in FCP2.

## Test Categories

### Ed25519 Signatures
- [ ] Sign/verify roundtrip
- [ ] Verify with wrong key fails
- [ ] Verify with tampered message fails
- [ ] Signature malleability checks
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 8032

### X25519 Key Exchange
- [ ] ECDH produces same shared secret both ways
- [ ] Reject low-order points
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 7748

### HPKE Sealing (RFC 9180)
- [ ] Seal/unseal roundtrip
- [ ] Unseal with wrong key fails
- [ ] AAD binding is enforced
- [ ] Multiple recipients (sealed to multiple keys)
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 9180

### HKDF-SHA256
- [ ] Extract/expand produces correct output
- [ ] Context/info binding changes output
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 5869

### ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD
- [ ] Encrypt/decrypt roundtrip
- [ ] Tampered ciphertext fails authentication
- [ ] Tampered AAD fails authentication
- [ ] Nonce reuse detection (if stateful)
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 8439

### XChaCha20-Poly1305 (if used)
- [ ] Extended nonce handling
- [ ] Same test matrix as ChaCha20-Poly1305

### BLAKE3 Hashing
- [ ] Hash produces expected output
- [ ] Keyed MAC mode
- [ ] Incremental hashing
- [ ] Test vectors from BLAKE3 spec

### COSE_Sign1 Helpers
- [ ] Create/verify COSE_Sign1 structure
- [ ] Protected headers in AAD
- [ ] KID extraction
- [ ] Invalid COSE structures rejected
- [ ] Test vectors from RFC 9052

### KID (Key ID) Generation
- [ ] KID format is stable
- [ ] Same key produces same KID
- [ ] Different keys produce different KIDs
- [ ] KID truncation is correct (8 bytes)

## Golden Vectors
Each category MUST have golden vectors:
- Input bytes (hex)
- Expected output bytes (hex)
- Source reference (RFC, test suite, etc.)

## Fuzz Targets
- COSE structure parsing
- HPKE unsealing (malformed inputs)
- Signature verification (malformed signatures)

## Logging Requirements
All tests emit structured JSON logs with:
- `test_name`
- `primitive` (ed25519, x25519, etc.)
- `operation` (sign, verify, seal, etc.)
- `result` (pass/fail)
- `vector_source` (RFC reference or "generated")

## Acceptance Criteria
- All crypto operations have test coverage
- Golden vectors exist and match
- Fuzz targets run without panics
- No secret material in test logs

@@ -914,7 +914,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7ca","title":"fcp.sentry: Release tracking and deployment correlation","description":"# fcp.sentry: Release tracking and deployment correlation (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to correlate production changes with incidents by accessing:
- releases
- deployments
- release health

## Scope
### Read operations
- List releases for a project/org with filters.
- Get release details:
  - version
  - commits (when available)
  - associated issues
  - deployment environment timeline
- Optional: fetch release health summaries (sessions, crash-free users).

### Write operations (optional; dangerous)
- Create a release marker or deployment marker.
  - Typically CI-owned; allow only if policy explicitly enables.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.releases.read`
- `sentry.releases.write` (dangerous; optional)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Avoid leaking commit metadata or PII via logs.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.release.list`
  - `sentry.release.get`
  - `sentry.release.health_summary`
  - Optional: `sentry.release.create`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - list/get pagination
  - association parsing
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - capability gating for any write ops

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can reliably answer: “what changed before this incident?”
- All writes (if enabled) are approval/capability gated and audited.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:15:01.085629953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T18:48:36.050515Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T18:48:36.050499Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7ca","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7ca","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1296,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7ca","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented release tracking operations: release.health (crash-free rates, adoption, sessions) and release.create (version, refs, date range, projects). Sentry connector now at 26 operations. All clippy clean, golden vectors pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T18:48:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","title":"[tooling] break fcp-core/fcp-testkit/fcp-conformance path cycle blocking rch remote verification","description":"Verbose `rch exec` preflight now points at a real workspace dependency-planner blocker rather than pure worker flakiness: `RCH-E326` reports a cyclic path dependency rooted at `crates/fcp-core`, with the current test-helper graph running through `fcp-core` dev-dep -> `fcp-testkit` -> `fcp-conformance` -> `fcp-core`, plus a direct `fcp-conformance` dev-dep -> `fcp-testkit` loop because conformance tests use `fcp_testkit::LogCapture` while `fcp-testkit` library code imports `fcp_conformance::schemas::*`. This can force `rch exec -- cargo ...` into forbidden local fail-open even for otherwise unrelated beads. Acceptance: 1) the path-cycle is removed or isolated so dependency preflight can construct an acyclic closure for normal repo-local remote cargo verification, 2) `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-kernel --lib` and comparable commands no longer fail-open locally due to this cycle, 3) the fix preserves needed test-helper functionality without reintroducing a hidden cycle elsewhere.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SageDuck","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:28:13.189515Z","created_by":"CoralSquirrel","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:49:28.850807Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T18:49:28.850603Z","close_reason":"Resolved dependency cycle; remaining rch failures are worker toolchain drift, not planner-cycle fail-open","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deps","rch","tooling","verification","workspace"],"comments":[{"id":2098,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Narrowed remaining RCH-E326 path cycle after the core/conformance helper split. Remote preflight no longer reports fcp-core; it now reports fcp-testkit. Current exact loop is store-side: fcp-store still has a dev-dependency on fcp-testkit, and internal test modules in crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs and crates/fcp-store/src/repair.rs only use it for LogCapture. Reserved Cargo.toml + symbol_store.rs; repair.rs is currently reserved by AzureOriole, so coordination is in progress before the final edge removal patch.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:39:46Z"},{"id":2099,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Retested after removing the direct fcp-testkit -> fcp-store edge. RCH still reports the same package-level cycle at fcp-testkit, which implies the planner is treating optional path edges as part of the exact closure. Remaining unavoidable fix is store-side: remove fcp-store's dev-dependency on fcp-testkit by replacing the two internal LogCapture uses in crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs and crates/fcp-store/src/repair.rs with a tiny local helper. Escalated to current owner AzureOriole with ack requested before conflicting edits.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:42:40Z"},{"id":2100,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Progress update: non-conflicting half of the store-side cycle fix is now in place. crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs no longer imports fcp_testkit::LogCapture, and crates/fcp-store/Cargo.toml now includes jsonschema for the local schema check helper. Remaining work is narrow: convert the single LogCapture use in crates/fcp-store/src/repair.rs and then remove fcp-testkit from fcp-store dev-dependencies before re-running rch remote verification.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:44:26Z"},{"id":2102,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Cut the final remaining host/e2e path cycle by removing the fcp-e2e dev-dependency from crates/fcp-host, localizing the tiny subprocess runner helper into crates/fcp-host/tests/host_connector_integration.rs, and replacing the host log schema example with a direct JSON payload. Fresh compliant remote verification is now in flight through rch; this run has already advanced past the former RCH-E326 fail-open point, selected worker vmi1153651, submitted job j-29772074671145636, and begun exact dependency-closure sync instead of falling back to local compilation.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:58:57Z"},{"id":2103,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Cut the final remaining host/e2e path cycle by removing  from  dev-dependencies, localizing the tiny subprocess runner helper into , and replacing the host log schema example with a direct JSON payload. Fresh compliant verification is now in flight via ; importantly, this run has already advanced past the former  fail-open point, selected worker , submitted remote job , and begun exact dependency-closure sync instead of falling back to local cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-29T03:06:10Z"},{"id":2104,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verification update: the old RCH-E326 planner failure is gone. After the host/e2e edge removal, rch successfully verified the exact remote dependency closure for 188 roots on worker vmi1153651 and launched remote cargo check for crates/fcp-host tests. That remote command then exceeded the 300 second wrapper and tried to fail open locally; I interrupted the local fallback immediately with Ctrl-C. A follow-up probe using rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-kernel --lib also selected the same worker and got past planner/job submission, but because this repo is dirty across many sync roots it began the full exact-closure sync again before execution, so I stopped that redundant second run. Current state: the path-cycle bug is fixed, and the remaining pain is rch full-sync plus timeout behavior on this dirty multi-root workspace rather than any remaining cargo metadata cycle in the project graph.","created_at":"2026-03-29T03:10:55Z"},{"id":2133,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"SilverQuarry","text":"2026-03-30 Codex verification pass. Repo-root rch cargo check on fcp-kernel prepared 188 roots and entered remote sync across sibling asupersync repos and many connector roots. I stopped that run once it was clear the bottleneck was closure-sync breadth rather than the old metadata cycle. Crate-scoped rch cargo check from crates/fcp-kernel prepared 11 roots, finished sync in about 20.8 seconds, and started remote cargo on worker vmi1149989. Remote cargo then failed because worker vmi1149989 is missing toolchain nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, after which rch tried to fall back locally. I stopped the local fallback immediately. Conclusion from this session: the old path-cycle planner blocker does not reproduce; remaining pain is exact-closure sync breadth at repo root plus worker toolchain drift and local fail-open behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:33Z"},{"id":2169,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"WildTern","text":"Fresh 2026-03-31 verification while validating a narrow tbjdk host-kernel slice. Results split by crate. Crate-scoped RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-kernel --tests from crates/fcp-kernel selected worker vmi1149989, verified an exact 11-root remote dependency closure, completed sync, and reached remote cargo, so the old planner blocker does not reproduce on that path. That remote cargo then failed for worker-image drift because nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is missing on vmi1149989, and I interrupted the forbidden local fallback immediately. However, crate-scoped RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --lib and --tests from crates/fcp-host still reproduce dependency-preflight fail-open RCH-E326, with the resolver reporting a cyclic path dependency at crates/fcp-conformance. Remote cargo never launches on that path, and I interrupted both local fallbacks immediately. Net: 7doo8 should remain open and narrowed as a still-reproducible host-conformance planner-cycle blocker rather than being treated as fully resolved repo-wide.","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:34:10Z"},{"id":2175,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"WildTern","text":"WildTern taking a narrow 7doo8 slice after manifest archaeology. Planned fix surface: crates/fcp-host/Cargo.toml, crates/fcp-store/Cargo.toml, crates/fcp-mesh/Cargo.toml, and crates/fcp-mesh/tests/mesh_integration.rs. Rationale: fcp-host still carries an unused direct dev-dependency on fcp-conformance; fcp-store still carries a stale dev-dependency on fcp-testkit with no remaining code references; and fcp-mesh still depends on fcp-testkit only for LogCapture in integration tests, which keeps a conformance -> mesh -> testkit -> conformance cycle alive under rch dependency preflight. I will localize the mesh test log capture helper and remove only those now-unneeded manifest edges.","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:44:09Z"},{"id":2176,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7doo8","author":"WildTern","text":"Completion evidence for 7doo8 after the narrow cycle-cut patch. Changes made: removed the unused direct fcp-conformance dev-dependency from crates/fcp-host/Cargo.toml; removed the stale unused fcp-testkit dev-dependency from crates/fcp-store/Cargo.toml; removed the fcp-testkit dev-dependency from crates/fcp-mesh/Cargo.toml; and localized the mesh integration LogCapture helper inside crates/fcp-mesh/tests/mesh_integration.rs using local tracing-subscriber plus jsonschema validation against the existing E2E log schema include path. Hygiene: rustfmt on the touched Rust file passed and git diff --check on the touched files passed. Remote-only verification now clears the old planner blocker: crate-scoped RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --lib and --tests both verified a 26-root remote dependency closure on worker vmi1153651 and reached remote cargo execution instead of failing preflight with RCH-E326. Both remote cargo runs then failed for a different reason, namely worker toolchain drift: nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is missing on vmi1153651. I interrupted both forbidden local fallbacks immediately. This means the cycle blocker described by 7doo8 is resolved; remaining failures belong to worker-image or toolchain-drift issues, not to this repo dependency cycle.","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:49:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7dx4z","title":"[fcp-host] Enable kill_on_drop for managed connector subprocess runner","description":"crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs claims to prevent zombie leaks in ConnectorProcessRunner::drop(), but it only calls Child::start_kill(). The underlying asupersync Child docs state start_kill/kill do not wait and still require wait() to reap. Mirror the e2e fix here by spawning with kill_on_drop(true) and removing the misleading manual Drop path once the current file reservation conflict is resolved.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:58:27.010925Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:57:36.737133Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:57:36.736936Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current HEAD; verified kill_on_drop host runner and regression test via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-7e15h","title":"fcp-e2e: ~100 compliance tests mount MockApiServer but don't assert mock was hit","description":"Survey of crates/fcp-e2e/tests/*_compliance_e2e.rs (99 files use
MockApiServer / wiremock). Only ONE of them — anthropic_compliance_e2e.rs
(2 call sites) — actually asserts that the mock HTTP server received
the expected request. Every other compliance test mounts a mock,
runs the connector suite, and asserts on the runner's returned
report.passed flag, but never independently verifies that the
connector's code path actually produced the expected network
side-effect.

Scan command:
  grep -c 'assert_request_count\|assert_received\|received_requests\|\
assert_no_requests' crates/fcp-e2e/tests/*.rs | awk -F: '$2>0'
  → crates/fcp-e2e/tests/anthropic_compliance_e2e.rs:2

Failure mode this gap masks:
A connector that short-circuits invoke — returns Ok without making
the HTTP call, e.g. because a cache layer or a local policy check
returned early — will still produce report.passed == true, since
E2eRunner::run_connector_suite at fcp-e2e/src/lib.rs:788 only
inspects the InvokeResponse status, not network side-effects.

Infrastructure already exists:
MockApiServer exposes assert_request_count, assert_received,
assert_no_requests, and received_requests() at
crates/fcp-testkit/src/mock_server.rs:235-278. Tests just don't
use them.

Proposed pattern (suggested inline here to preserve the work
done during this audit):

    let received = mock.received_requests().await;
    let hits = received.iter().filter(|r| {
        r.method == wiremock::http::Method::POST
            && r.url.path() == \"/expected/endpoint\"
    }).count();
    assert_eq!(hits, 1, \"expected exactly one POST to /endpoint\");

    // And verify body fields the connector must not drop:
    let body: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&received[0].body).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(body.get(\"chat_id\"), Some(&json!(\"987654321\")));

Blocked on: flywheel_connectors-1maun (the telegram allow-path test
is currently failing at the existing assert!(report.passed), so
strengthening it now would stack failures). Once 1maun lands,
telegram is the natural first target — its happy-path test is
simple, and the bot-token POST pattern generalizes to every REST
connector. Filing as P2 because the gap is systemic but not
immediately exploitable.","notes":"P2 slice landed for slack/github/stripe/telegram/discord. Added MockApiServer hit assertions after report.passed for each allow-path test. Also fixed missing constraints CBOR in the e2e token helpers these tests rely on; github lane also needed OAuthError::EmptyTokenField(_) mapped so the crate compiled. Remaining sweep still open for the other compliance tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:50:41.261391Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:18:10.751851Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7e15h","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1maun","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:51:02.754186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","title":"fcp.openai - OpenAI Provider Connector","description":"# fcp.openai - OpenAI Provider Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant OpenAI provider connector (chat, embeddings, audio, images) with streaming support, usage/cost metrics (no embedded pricing tables), and strict single-zone + default-deny enforcement.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 94/100** — Critical Tier 1 connector (AI Provider family).

**Why Critical**: OpenAI offers the broadest suite of AI capabilities including chat completions, embeddings, vision, audio (Whisper, TTS), and image generation (DALL-E). Essential for multi-modal agent workflows.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Foundation)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 1 Foundation (immediate)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec![ConnectorArchetype::Operational, ConnectorArchetype::Streaming]
```
- **Operational**: Standard request/response for chat, embeddings, images
- **Streaming**: SSE streaming for chat completions

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: No mesh-persisted state needed for most operations
- Assistants API threads are managed by OpenAI, not connector state
- Each request is independent

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: External API connector with credential handling
- Memory isolation for API key safety
- Cross-platform consistent behavior

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    host_allow: vec!["api.openai.com".into()],
    port_allow: vec![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
AI model outputs carry provenance metadata:
```rust
// Model outputs are internally generated
model_output.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::NONE;

// If output contains URLs:
if output.contains_urls {
    model_output.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}

// Outputs that will be interpreted by LLM:
if output.is_prompt_surface {
    model_output.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::PROMPT_SURFACE;
}
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `openai.chat.completions` | Safe | Read-only generation |
| `openai.chat.stream` | Safe | Read-only streaming |
| `openai.embeddings.create` | Safe | Read-only embedding |
| `openai.audio.transcription` | Safe | Read-only transcription |
| `openai.audio.speech` | Risky | Resource-intensive |
| `openai.images.generate` | Risky | High-cost, content risk |
| `openai.finetune.manage` | Dangerous | Resource-intensive, billing |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Text & Chat
- Chat completions (GPT-4, GPT-4o, etc.)
- Function calling / tool use
- Assistants API for stateful conversations
- JSON mode for structured outputs

### Embeddings
- Text embeddings for semantic search
- Document embedding for RAG pipelines
- Code embeddings

### Audio
- Whisper for transcription
- TTS for text-to-speech
- Voice mode integration

### Vision & Image
- Vision (images in prompts)
- DALL-E image generation
- Image editing

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Transcribe this audio file"
"Generate embeddings for these documents"
"Create an image for this concept"
"Analyze this image and extract data"
"Convert this text to speech"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### LLM Router Integration
- Part of the `fcp.llm-router` meta-connector strategy
- Fallback when Anthropic unavailable
- Different model strengths for different tasks

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
- **Embeddings API is critical for CASS**
- Generate embeddings for semantic indexing
- Power vector search across Flywheel

### Whisper Connector Synergy
- Can use OpenAI Whisper API
- Or local Whisper model via fcp.whisper
- Consistent interface either way

### Agent Mail
- TTS for audio notifications
- Transcribe voice messages

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **Chat Completions** — Primary text generation
- **Embeddings** — Vector generation
- **Audio** — Whisper transcription, TTS
- **Images** — DALL-E generation/editing
- **Assistants** — Stateful conversation threads
- **Fine-tuning** — Model customization management

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client with streaming
- `async-sse` — SSE parsing for streaming
- `serde_json` — JSON handling
- `base64` — Audio/image encoding

### Streaming Implementation
Similar to Anthropic, uses SSE:
```rust
// data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"token"}}]}
```

### Assistants API
- Threads maintain conversation state
- Runs execute assistant on thread
- File attachments for documents
- Code interpreter tool

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ai:openai:full` | Full access | All models, all capabilities |
| `z:ai:openai:gpt4` | GPT-4 access | GPT-4 models only |
| `z:ai:openai:gpt35` | GPT-3.5 access | Cost-effective option |
| `z:ai:openai:embeddings` | Embeddings only | No generation |
| `z:ai:openai:audio` | Audio only | Whisper + TTS |
| `z:ai:openai:images` | Images only | DALL-E |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — Standard request/response
- `streaming` — SSE streaming for completions

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.openai.com:443`

### Credential Injection
- API key via `CredentialId`
- Organization ID (optional) via config
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Text Generation
- `openai.chat.completions` — Chat completions
- `openai.chat.stream` — Streaming completions

### Embeddings
- `openai.embeddings.create` — Generate embeddings

### Audio
- `openai.audio.transcription` — Whisper transcription
- `openai.audio.translation` — Whisper translation
- `openai.audio.speech` — TTS generation

### Images (Dangerous - Cost)
- `openai.images.generate` — DALL-E generation
- `openai.images.edit` — Image editing
- `openai.images.variations` — Image variations

### Assistants
- `openai.assistants.manage` — Create/update assistants
- `openai.threads.manage` — Manage conversation threads
- `openai.runs.execute` — Execute assistant runs

### Fine-tuning (Dangerous)
- `openai.finetune.manage` — Manage fine-tuning jobs

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Chat Completions
```json
POST /chat/completions
{
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "messages": [...],
  "tools": [...],
  "response_format": {"type": "json_object"},
  "stream": true
}
```

### Embeddings
```json
POST /embeddings
{
  "model": "text-embedding-3-small",
  "input": ["text to embed"]
}
```

### Audio Transcription
```json
POST /audio/transcriptions
{
  "model": "whisper-1",
  "file": <audio_file>,
  "response_format": "verbose_json"
}
```

### Image Generation
```json
POST /images/generations
{
  "model": "dall-e-3",
  "prompt": "...",
  "size": "1024x1024",
  "quality": "hd"
}
```

---

## Safety + Retries

### Rate Limits
- Different limits per model/capability
- Respect `retry-after` headers
- Implement token-bucket rate limiting

### Cost Control
- Track tokens per request
- Image generation is expensive
- Budget enforcement at zone level

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request construction for all API endpoints
- Response parsing (including streaming)
- Token counting
- Error handling

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based API simulation
- Multi-modal scenarios (text + image)
- Embedding dimension verification

### E2E Compliance
- Validates default deny + NetworkConstraints
- Usage metrics accurate
- Cost tracking functional

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real OpenAI calls in unit tests
- All major APIs functional (chat, embeddings, audio, images)
- Streaming works correctly
- Tool use functions properly
- Usage metrics emitted
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:12:25.148947482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:21:30.317177Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T03:21:30.317161Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9co","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7hb.1","title":"fcp.openai: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.openai: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Specify the OpenAI connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`operational`, `streaming`)
- operation list + schema IDs
- capability families + per-operation required caps
- NetworkConstraints (default-deny; explicit allowlist)
- state model (likely stateless for most ops; stateful only where required)

## Key design choices to lock down
- How we represent “model identifier” in schemas and provenance.
- How we represent streaming responses (chunk schema, final summary schema).
- Which operations are considered side-effecting and require OperationIntent/Receipt.
- Which operations are Dangerous (fine-tuning, file writes) and require ApprovalToken `Execution` scope.

## Outputs
- Complete manifest section stored in this bead.
- Good/bad manifest examples for static compliance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","title":"fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management","description":"# fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to manage saved posts/comments for research workflows:
- save/unsave
- list saved items

## Scope
- Save a post/comment.
- Unsave a post/comment.
- List saved items (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.saved.read`
- `reddit.saved.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts/audit for write operations.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.saved.list`
  - `reddit.saved.save`
  - `reddit.saved.unsave`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Saved-items workflows are reliable and auditable.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwcci","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-oauth PKCE code_verifier overwritten by extra_token_params","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:541-548 (introduced/touched 76bb8311) — in exchange_code_internal the Pkce argument inserts 'code_verifier' into params at line 542, but the subsequent loop over self.config.extra_token_params (lines 546-548) unconditionally calls params.insert(key, value.clone()), which WILL overwrite the code_verifier from the real Pkce if the caller happened to put a stale/empty 'code_verifier' in extra_token_params. This is both a silent bug (correctness) and a latent security hole — the PKCE gate at line 525 treats presence of the key in extra_token_params as satisfying PKCE, so an attacker who can influence extra_token_params could short-circuit real PKCE. Suggested fix: apply Pkce last so it wins; or explicitly reject 'code_verifier' as an allowed extra_token_params key; or validate the non-empty structure of the verifier (43-128 chars, RFC 7636). Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:45:15.459460Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:45:15.459460Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwcci","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-oauth PKCE code_verifier overwritten by extra_token_params","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:541-548 (introduced/touched 76bb8311) — in exchange_code_internal the Pkce argument inserts 'code_verifier' into params at line 542, but the subsequent loop over self.config.extra_token_params (lines 546-548) unconditionally calls params.insert(key, value.clone()), which WILL overwrite the code_verifier from the real Pkce if the caller happened to put a stale/empty 'code_verifier' in extra_token_params. This is both a silent bug (correctness) and a latent security hole — the PKCE gate at line 525 treats presence of the key in extra_token_params as satisfying PKCE, so an attacker who can influence extra_token_params could short-circuit real PKCE. Suggested fix: apply Pkce last so it wins; or explicitly reject 'code_verifier' as an allowed extra_token_params key; or validate the non-empty structure of the verifier (43-128 chars, RFC 7636). Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:45:15.459460Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:15:27.690539Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:15:27.690288Z","close_reason":"Moved extra_token_params loop BEFORE the PKCE code_verifier insert in exchange_code_internal so the honest PKCE verifier always wins on conflict. Added regression test test_exchange_code_pkce_wins_over_extra_token_params_code_verifier that plants a bogus code_verifier in extra_token_params and asserts the mock server receives the real Pkce.verifier() value. Verified: 611+15+59 tests pass in fcp-oauth.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.

Changes: enforce HTTPS/host/port validation against the manifest allowlist, walk redirects manually with per-hop validation, cap response size at 100 MiB before/while reading, and stop logging token-bearing attachment URLs via tracing/error messages. Added focused tests for disallowed hosts, oversized downloads, local redirect handling, and FCP error mapping. Validation completed: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-check cargo check -p fcp-airtable --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-test cargo test -p fcp-airtable --test integration -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-clippy cargo clippy -p fcp-airtable --all-targets -- -D warnings. Workspace-wide cargo check was attempted via rch but fell back locally and is still blocked/contended by concurrent cargo activity from other agents, so I am leaving the bead in_progress until that clears or someone explicitly accepts the package-scoped verification.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:50:36Z"},{"id":1306,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented attachments.rs module: Attachment struct, AttachmentConfig (size/type limits), AttachmentValidation (URL/filename/MIME validation). 110 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fytk7","title":"fcp-tailscale exports MockTailscaleClient from production lib surface","description":"`crates/fcp-tailscale/src/lib.rs` publicly reexports `MockTailscaleClient`, and `crates/fcp-tailscale/src/client.rs` declares it as a normal `pub struct` with public constructors/helpers. The only consumers are crate-local unit tests and `crates/fcp-tailscale/tests/no_mock_integration.rs`, so downstream production code can import a pure in-memory fake client today with no explicit test-only gate.

Suggested fix: add an explicit `test-mocks` feature, gate `MockTailscaleClient` and its `lib.rs` reexport behind `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-mocks"))]`, and require that feature for the integration test target so production builds no longer expose the mock surface by default.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:34:15.215012Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:28.943793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:28.943564Z","close_reason":"Default builds no longer export MockTailscaleClient; no_mock_integration now opts in via test-mocks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp-tailscale","security","test-hygiene"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","title":"fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide fast, typed Spotify search for discovery workflows.

## Scope
- Search across types:
  - track
  - album
  - artist
  - playlist
  - show/podcast
  - episode
- Support filters:
  - market/locale
  - pagination

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.search.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Spotify Web API host.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.search`
  - `spotify.search.tracks`
  - `spotify.search.albums`
  - `spotify.search.artists`
  - `spotify.search.shows`
  - `spotify.search.episodes`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Search results are stable and correctly typed.

All with optional market parameter. 113 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:33Z"},{"id":1358,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented search.rs: SearchQuery builder, SearchPage<T> generic pagination, typed results for Track/Album/Artist/Playlist/Show/Episode, SearchValidator. 151 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:45:44Z"}]}
